title: austrofestuca littoralis
intro: Austrofestuca littoralis, also known as grass tree or beach grass tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae, native to Australia and New Zealand. The medicinal properties of Austrofestuca littoralis have been used for centuries by indigenous Australians to treat various health issues, including sore throats, coughs, and skin irritations, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe respiratory problems, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Austrofestuca littoralis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and stems are used in traditional herbal remedies to treat various ailments.
